name: Repair Cost
description: Repair Cost is a value-exchange mechanic that governs currency, prices, scarcity, rewards, and transactions.
design_notes: Repair Cost should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For economy mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use repair cost as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect repair cost to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are making meaningful tradeoffs with scarce value.

parameters.name: scarcity_modifier
parameters.description: Multiplier that reflects rarity, supply, or demand.

parameters.name: transaction_limit
parameters.description: Maximum units exchanged in one operation or period.

edge_cases: Repair Cost is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Repair Cost overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Repair Cost runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Repair Cost state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Repair Cost plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Repair Cost produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Repair Cost strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Repair Cost becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Repair Cost with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Repair Cost as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Repair Cost with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Repair Cost as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
